Recent papers

Recent papers on Landau's problems on prime numbers, surfaced by the prominence of their authors in this directory.

Showing 15 papers, ranked by the Top 100 standing of their authors then by recency. Authors who appear in the Top 100 are shown in bold.

#TitleAuthorsPublishedBest rank
1The average number of representations of an integer as a sum of two prime powers over multiples of a fixed integerAlessandra Migliaccio, Alessandro Zaccagnini2026-03#3
2Theorem $(1+1.9)$ on the Goldbach ConjectureJiamin Li, Jianya Liu2026-06#4
3Rough numbers between consecutive primesAyla Gafni, Terence Tao2025-08#15
4Diophantine approximation with primes from short intervalsStephan Baier, Sayantan Roy2025-12#16
5Jacob's ladders and the equivalent of the Fermat-Wiles theorem generated by the Hardy-Littlewood formula (1921)Jan Moser2026-03#20
6Jacob's ladders, our asymptotic formulae (1981) and next $ζ$-equivalents of the Fermat-Wiles theorem together with decomposition and synthesis of the Riemann's $ζ$-oscillatorsJan Moser2025-08#20
7Proof of a conjecture of Garvan and Jennings-Shaffer on the nonnegativity of M_{C1}(m,n) and M_{C5}(m,n)Bing He, Shuming Liu2025-09#31
8The Exceptional Set in Goldbach's Problem with two Chen PrimesLasse Grimmelt, Joni Teräväinen2025-08#33
9Beyond the square-root barrier: cubic forms of Perazzo typeTim Browning, Ritabrata Munshi, Victor Y. Wang2026-04#35
10Distinction between hyper-Kloosterman sums and multiplicative functionsYang Zhang2025-10#43
11On the exceptional set in the $abc$ conjectureRunbo Li2025-06#51
12The reverse Goldbach problem and a refined Zsiflaw--Legeis theoremMichael Harm, Daniel R. Johnston2026-05#54
13An update on the Linnik--Goldbach and Romanov problemsDaniel R. Johnston, Tim Trudgian2026-05#54
14Primes and almost primes between cubesDaniel R. Johnston, Jonathan P. Sorenson, Simon N. Thomas, Jonathan E. Webster2026-01#54
15Strict Log-concavity of $k$-coloured PartitionsKathrin Bringmann, Ben Kane, Anubhab Pahari, Larry Rolen2025-10#65

Most cited (all time)

The ten most-cited Mathematics works on Landau's problems on prime numbers, by citation count in OpenAlex. Results are filtered to the Mathematics field (OpenAlex field 26) and ranked by total citations.

#TitleAuthorsYearCitations
1Bounded gaps between primesZhang Yitang2014463 citations
2Empirical verification of the even Goldbach conjecture and computation of prime gaps up to 4⋅10¹⁸Tomás Oliveira e Silva; Siegfried Herzog; Silvio Pardi2013165 citations
3The ternary Goldbach conjecture is trueH. A. Helfgott201399 citations
4Verifying the Goldbach conjecture up to 4⋅10¹⁴J. Richstein200064 citations
5Irregularities in the distribution of primes and twin primesRichard P. Brent197555 citations
6Average twin prime conjecture for elliptic curvesAntal Balog; Alina-Carmen Cojocaru; Chantal David201150 citations
7Evaluation of Artin’s constant and the twin-prime constantJohn W. Wrench196148 citations
8Chen's double sieve, Goldbach's conjecture and the twin prime problemJie Wu200447 citations
9Bounded gaps between primes with a given primitive rootPaul Pollack201439 citations
10On the Vinogradov bound in the three primes Goldbach conjectureMengmeng Liu; Tianze Wang200236 citations
